content_standards.statement: Demonstrate the ability to listen attentively and respond appropriately to spoken texts in a variety of contexts.

indicators.statement: Listen to a passage read aloud and answer comprehension questions to demonstrate understanding of main ideas and supporting details.
exemplars: Listen to a short narrative and retell the story in own words.
Answer who, what, when, where, why and how questions after listening to a passage.

indicators.statement: Listen to spoken instructions and carry out tasks accurately, demonstrating understanding of sequence markers (first, next, then, finally).
exemplars: Follow a set of oral instructions to complete a task.
Identify sequence markers in spoken instructions and restate steps in order.

content_standards.statement: Speak clearly and fluently in English using appropriate grammar, vocabulary and pronunciation in various communicative situations.

indicators.statement: Introduce oneself and others formally and informally, using appropriate register, greetings and conversational phrases.
exemplars: Role-play formal and informal introduction scenarios.
Practise greetings and polite conversational phrases in pairs.

indicators.statement: Describe people, places and objects using appropriate adjectives and descriptive language in well-structured sentences.
exemplars: Describe a familiar place using sensory details (sight, sound, smell).
Present an oral description of a person using physical and character adjectives.

indicators.statement: Narrate personal experiences and events chronologically using past tense and appropriate time connectives.
exemplars: Tell the class about a memorable holiday experience.
Use connectives (first, afterwards, eventually) to sequence events in oral narration.

content_standards.statement: Participate effectively in group discussions and debates, expressing and supporting opinions with reasons and evidence.

indicators.statement: Contribute relevant ideas during group discussions, taking turns to speak and listening respectfully to others' viewpoints.
exemplars: Participate in a group discussion on a given topic and summarise key points.
Practise turn-taking and active listening in small group conversations.

indicators.statement: Present arguments for and against a given topic in a simple debate format using persuasive language.
exemplars: Prepare and present a 2-minute argument for or against a motion.
Use phrases such as 'I believe', 'In my opinion', 'On the other hand' in debates.

content_standards.statement: Read and comprehend a variety of texts (narratives, expository, descriptive) identifying main ideas, supporting details and the author's purpose.

indicators.statement: Read a given passage silently and identify the main idea, topic sentences and supporting details in each paragraph.
exemplars: Underline topic sentences and supporting details in a passage.
Write a one-sentence summary for each paragraph of a text.

indicators.statement: Use context clues (definition, synonym, antonym, example) to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words in a text.
exemplars: Identify context clues in sentences and infer word meanings.
Match unfamiliar words to their meanings using clues from surrounding text.

indicators.statement: Make inferences and draw conclusions from information stated and implied in a text.
exemplars: Answer inferential questions about a fiction or non-fiction passage.
Distinguish between what the text says explicitly and what can be inferred.

content_standards.statement: Build and apply a rich vocabulary using word formation processes and reference materials.

indicators.statement: Use prefixes (un-, re-, dis-, pre-, mis-) and suffixes (-tion, -ness, -ful, -less, -ment) to form new words and determine their meanings.
exemplars: Add prefixes and suffixes to root words and explain how meaning changes.
Sort words by their prefixes or suffixes and define them.

indicators.statement: Identify and use synonyms, antonyms and homophones correctly in sentences.
exemplars: Match words with their synonyms and antonyms.
Use homophones (their/there/they're, right/write) correctly in sentences.

content_standards.statement: Read aloud with fluency, accuracy, appropriate pace, expression and intonation.

indicators.statement: Read grade-level passages aloud with correct pronunciation, appropriate pausing at punctuation marks and natural expression.
exemplars: Practise choral and echo reading to build fluency.
Record and self-assess oral reading for pace and expression.

content_standards.statement: Identify and use the eight parts of speech correctly in sentences.

indicators.statement: Identify and classify nouns (common, proper, collective, abstract, concrete) and use them correctly in sentences.
exemplars: Sort nouns into categories: common, proper, collective, abstract.
Write sentences using different types of nouns.

indicators.statement: Identify and use personal, possessive, reflexive and demonstrative pronouns correctly, ensuring pronoun-antecedent agreement.
exemplars: Replace nouns with appropriate pronouns in given sentences.
Identify the antecedent for each pronoun in a paragraph.

indicators.statement: Use adjectives (descriptive, comparative, superlative) and adverbs (manner, time, place, frequency) to modify nouns and verbs.
exemplars: Form comparative and superlative adjectives (big, bigger, biggest).
Identify adverbs in sentences and classify them by type.

indicators.statement: Identify and use prepositions and prepositional phrases correctly to show relationships of time, place and direction.
exemplars: Underline prepositions and prepositional phrases in given texts.
Write sentences using prepositions of time (at, on, in) and place (beside, between, among).

content_standards.statement: Demonstrate understanding and correct use of verb tenses in writing and speech.

indicators.statement: Use the simple present, simple past and simple future tenses correctly in sentences, distinguishing their functions.
exemplars: Write paragraphs using each simple tense and explain when to use each.
Change sentences from one tense to another.

indicators.statement: Use the present continuous, past continuous and future continuous tenses to express ongoing actions at specific times.
exemplars: Describe actions happening now, that were happening and that will be happening.
Identify continuous tenses in a reading passage.

indicators.statement: Form and use the present perfect and past perfect tenses correctly, understanding their relationship to time.
exemplars: Write sentences using 'have/has + past participle' and 'had + past participle'.
Distinguish between simple past and present perfect usage.

content_standards.statement: Construct grammatically correct simple, compound and complex sentences.

indicators.statement: Identify and construct simple sentences with subject-verb-object structure and ensure subject-verb agreement.
exemplars: Identify the subject and predicate in sentences.
Correct subject-verb agreement errors in given sentences.

indicators.statement: Form compound sentences using coordinating conjunctions (FANBOYS: for, and, nor, but, or, yet, so) and appropriate punctuation.
exemplars: Join simple sentences using coordinating conjunctions.
Punctuate compound sentences correctly with commas before conjunctions.

indicators.statement: Construct complex sentences using subordinating conjunctions (because, although, when, if, while, since, after, before).
exemplars: Combine sentences using subordinating conjunctions.
Identify main and subordinate clauses in complex sentences.

content_standards.statement: Write well-structured narrative compositions with a clear beginning, middle and end, using descriptive language and dialogue.

indicators.statement: Plan and write a narrative essay with an engaging introduction, well-developed body paragraphs with events in chronological order, and a satisfying conclusion.
exemplars: Use a story map/plan to organise ideas before writing.
Write a narrative on 'My Most Memorable Day' with vivid descriptions.

indicators.statement: Use dialogue, descriptive adjectives and figurative language (similes) to make narratives more interesting and vivid.
exemplars: Include at least two exchanges of dialogue with correct punctuation in a story.
Use similes and descriptive words to enhance narrative writing.

content_standards.statement: Write descriptive essays and paragraphs that paint a vivid picture using sensory details and spatial organisation.

indicators.statement: Describe a person, place or object using sensory details (sight, sound, touch, taste, smell) organised logically.
exemplars: Write a descriptive paragraph about the school compound.
Describe a favourite food using all five senses.

content_standards.statement: Write formal and informal letters following the correct format, tone and conventions.

indicators.statement: Write an informal letter to a friend or relative, using the correct format (address, date, salutation, body, subscription) and an appropriate friendly tone.
exemplars: Write a letter to a friend describing a recent event.
Identify the parts of an informal letter from a sample.

indicators.statement: Write a formal letter (to a headteacher, editor or official) using the correct format and formal register.
exemplars: Write a letter to the headteacher requesting permission for a school trip.
Compare the format and language of formal and informal letters.

content_standards.statement: Apply correct spelling and punctuation rules consistently in all written work.

indicators.statement: Use capital letters, full stops, commas, question marks, exclamation marks and apostrophes (possession and contraction) correctly.
exemplars: Punctuate unpunctuated passages correctly.
Distinguish between apostrophes for possession and contraction.

content_standards.statement: Identify and appreciate the major genres of literature: prose, poetry and drama.

indicators.statement: Distinguish between prose, poetry and drama, identifying the key features and characteristics of each genre.
exemplars: Read samples of prose, poetry and drama and list distinguishing features.
Classify given texts into their literary genres with reasons.

indicators.statement: Identify basic literary devices (simile, metaphor, personification, alliteration) in poems and prose passages.
exemplars: Find examples of similes and metaphors in given poems.
Create own sentences using personification and alliteration.
